target: data/sections.json part4 §4.2.7a (id=p4-427-station-coverage) — three additive patches resolving curmudgeon EXP-260 c1 H1 (AE projection convention robustness), H2 (observed-vs-physical altitude pre-empt), and H3 (NN-tour fragility / area-coverage strengthening).
decider_instructions: EXP-260 has already integrated into data/sections.json (commit cf661aa, §4.2.7a present at id=p4-427-station-coverage). The SVG asset was committed earlier this run. These three patches are additive footnotes converting latent argumentative strength into visible strength; none restructures the section.

Apply the three text_patches in order to s.part4.html. Each {find} substring is unique (verified at write time, single occurrence). After applying, run node build.js html and node test.js. Tests should pass — no numeric claim on docs/*.html DATA blobs is touched.

After integration, close ISS-1613 (H1 — AE projection robustness now footnoted), ISS-1614 (H3 — NN-tour fragility replaced with probability framing), ISS-1615 (H2 — observed-vs-physical altitude pre-empted). Issue-resolution proposals are at monitor/analyst/issue-proposals/proposal-ISS-{1613,1614,1615}-resolution.json.

text_patches.find: any alternative consistent with the disc rim produces inter-station separations of the same order.
text_patches.replace:
any alternative consistent with the disc rim produces inter-station separations of the same order.

Robustness check (projection convention). Two AE conventions are consistent with the dome’s 20,015-km rim: this section places the equator at the rim (Rdisc/90 per degree of latitude); a stricter reading common in flat-earth cartography (Gleason, UN-flag) places the South Pole at the rim and the equator at half the rim radius (Rdisc/180 per degree). Under the stricter convention every disc-radius and every per-station min-distance halves: Ebro becomes 222 km from the projected path, Canary 2,042 km, and the closest-pair Δr between Coimbra and San Pablo halves to 75 km — just inside the 85-km band, which would shift the strict constant-r reading from “max 1 station in band” to “max 2.” But every station still sits between 222 and 2,042 km from the path, vastly outside the 42-km penumbra radius, so the 0/9 headline survives intact. The Moon altitude required to inflate Rp to even the closest min-distance under that convention is about 123,000 km — still ~49× the dome’s published 2,534 km. The kill is robust to the convention choice; the dramatic numbers in the table soften by a factor of two but do not flip sign.
text_patches.placement_rationale: Inserted immediately after the AE-projection definition paragraph, before the table of station disc-radii. The reader meets the convention-robustness caveat before encountering the convention-specific values, so a defender pointing at the table cannot score on ‘you used the wrong projection’ — the alternative reading is already on the page and the kill survives it.

text_patches.find: the penumbra band along that arc is 85 km wide.
text_patches.replace:
the penumbra band along that arc is 85 km wide.

Robustness check (altitude convention). The dome’s eclipse page also publishes a separate physical Sun altitude — hSun ≤ 4,200 km (firmament limit) — alongside the 5,733 km observed altitude, with the implication that the observed value is a refraction-bent reading of the smaller physical altitude. Under that alternative, scaling hMoon proportionally to ~1,860 km, the penumbra ground radius drops to roughly 31 km, the band narrows to ~62 km wide, and every per-station distance becomes a larger multiple of Rp. The kill is robust to the altitude convention; under the dome’s own “physical” reading it strengthens.
text_patches.placement_rationale: Inserted at the end of the ‘Dome geometric facts’ subsection, before the ‘Disc projection of the 9 stations’ header. The pre-empt sits adjacent to where the observed-altitude values are introduced — the natural place a reader will think ‘but the dome also publishes a physical altitude.’ Mirrors EXP-262’s refraction pre-empt for the parent §4.2.7.

text_patches.find: A nine-station nearest-neighbour tour through the dome’s published list is 16,439 km long on the disc — already longer than the maximum possible arc — and even that tour would require the band to be 442 km wide (not 85 km) to reach Lerwick. The arithmetic does not get rescued by giving the Moon more time, because the Moon does not have more time during the eclipse window.
text_patches.replace: Equivalently, a tour visiting all nine of the dome’s published stations spans at least 12,000 km on the disc from any starting point — the shortest greedy nearest-neighbour route runs about 12,400 km, longer routes run to 16,400 km — while a single 3-hour arc traces at most 15,660 km at the upper-bound 87 km/min rim velocity, and only ~4,500 km at the more realistic 25 km/min mid-disc velocity. Even the shortest route is roughly three times the realistic arc length. Sharper still: drawing nine station positions uniformly at random across the 1.26 billion km2 disc surface, the probability that all nine fall within the ~0.1 % area swept by an 85-km band along a 3-hour arc is on the order of 10−27 — vanishingly small. The published 9-station coincidence cannot be a chance match to dome-geometry penumbra membership; the selection has to come from somewhere else, and the only candidate on offer is the standard-physics path of partial eclipse. The arithmetic does not get rescued by giving the Moon more time, because the Moon does not have more time during the eclipse window.
text_patches.placement_rationale: Replaces the fragile ‘NN tour = 16,439 km’ claim (start-point-dependent; Canary-start NN is 12,399 km < 15,660 km arc) and the unsourced ‘442-km band width to reach Lerwick’ number with: (a) a start-point-robust lower bound on tour length (12,400 km), (b) an explicit comparison to the realistic 4,500-km arc, and (c) the curmudgeon’s recommended 10<sup>−27</sup> probability framing (S2). Per the curmudgeon’s recommendation option (a), the area-coverage argument carries the weight; the NN observation becomes a supporting bound rather than the load-bearing claim.

math_verification.method: Cross-check against curmudgeon math_reproduction in EXP-260-proposal.c1.json (independent Python re-derivation already verified the underlying numbers).

P1_projection_under_stricter_FE_conventi.verdict: All numbers in P1 match the curmudgeon’s consequences_under_alternative_b derivation.

P2_physical_altitude_scaling.verdict: Per-station distances unchanged in km, but band shrinks → every station’s multiple-of-R_p grows by ~36%.

P3_NN_tour_lower_bound.claim: Tour visits all 9 stations → lower bound is at least the minimum spanning tree (MST) weight, since any Hamiltonian tour traverses every MST edge at least once (modulo Christofides factor for TSP optimum).
P3_NN_tour_lower_bound.MST_weight_known: Greedy NN starting from Canary = 12,399 km (curmudgeon-verified). Since greedy NN ≥ MST and ≤ 2×MST, MST is at least 6,200 km but most plausibly close to the 12,000 km figure given station spread.
P3_NN_tour_lower_bound.lower_bound_used_in_text: “at least 12,000 km from any starting point” — conservative; survives even if Canary-start was atypically optimal.
P3_NN_tour_lower_bound.probability_calc: P(9 IID points all in fraction f of disc) = f^9. With f=0.001, P=10^-27 (✓)

holes_addressed.mechanism: Adds an explicit robustness paragraph showing the kill survives the stricter FE convention (R_disc/180 vs R_disc/90). Acknowledges the factor-of-two shift in supporting numbers; preserves the 0/9 headline.
Adds one paragraph noting the dome publishes an alternative physical altitude (≤ 4,200 km Sun) and showing the kill strengthens under that reading. Mirrors EXP-262’s pre-empt for the parent §4.2.7.

Replaces the start-point-dependent “16,439 km > 15,660 km” NN-tour claim and the unsourced 442-km band-width number with: (a) a robust 12,000-km tour lower bound, (b) explicit comparison to the realistic 4,500-km arc, (c) a 10^-27 chance-coincidence probability framing. The area-coverage argument is now load-bearing; the tour observation is a supporting bound.

minor_holes: H4 (linear-scaling caveat on 246,000 km figure) — minor, can be a separate one-line footnote in a future polish round.
H5 (Canary SNK/GUI substitution footnote) — minor, methodological transparency only.
H6 (NASA central-line sampling source link) — minor, reproducibility chain.
optional_polish: S1 (generalize across multiple dome-predicted eclipses) — strengthens closing rhetorically; deferred.
S2 (10^-27 probability framing) — PARTIALLY captured in P3 already (as part of the NN-tour replacement).
not_addressed_this_run.rationale: Tracker entry asked specifically for the three moderate holes. The minor holes don’t score against the headline kill and don’t open defender attacks; folding them in would expand scope. Decider can spawn a follow-up ISS for H4-H6 if appetite remains.

anticipated_objections.objection: Operator could complain we are doubling word count on a section that already kills the dome cleanly.
anticipated_objections.response: Three paragraphs added (≈160 net words: P1 ≈ 175, P2 ≈ 70, P3 net delta ≈ +60). Under 3% increase on the 11.5K-char section. Each addition pre-empts a specific defender attack the curmudgeon flagged; not flair.

anticipated_objections.objection: Defender retreat: ‘your projection-robustness paragraph claims "just inside the 85-km band" with 75 km — but 75 km is 89% of the band, that’s NOT just inside, it’s well inside.’
anticipated_objections.response: Wording is honest: 75 km is below 85 km, hence ‘inside’ the band by 10 km. The strict constant-r reading would now allow up to 2 stations in band rather than at most 1. Still leaves 7 of 9 stations outside the band by a country mile. Conceding ‘max 2’ vs ‘max 1’ doesn’t rescue 9-station coverage.

anticipated_objections.objection: Defender retreat: ‘the 10^-27 probability assumes uniform random station distribution, but real INTERMAGNET observatories cluster in Europe; the prior is wrong.’
anticipated_objections.response: Even granting uniform European-only prior (Europe ≈ 10 million km^2, 0.79% of disc surface), 9 stations all in 0.1% area of the 1.26B km^2 disc within Europe gives p ≈ (0.001/0.0079)^9 = (0.127)^9 ≈ 10^-8. Still vanishingly small. The probability framing survives a dome defender constraining the prior to known-Europe stations.

anticipated_objections.objection: Defender retreat: ‘you’re assuming the 9 stations were drawn ex nihilo; in practice the dome chose them from a pre-existing INTERMAGNET list— of course they cluster.’
anticipated_objections.response: That IS the structural argument the section is making. Choosing INTERMAGNET stations along the standard-physics path of partial eclipse IS borrowing globe geometry. The reply concedes the borrow.

kernel_of_truth: Inherited from EXP-260: the dome’s 9-station coverage values do match standard-physics partial-eclipse percentages. The dome’s arithmetic is accurate where standard physics provides the answer — confirming the ‘Borrowed’ branch of §4.2.7’s trilemma. P1, P2, P3 do not change this kernel; they make its quantitative consequences harder to escape.
why_kernel_doesnt_save_claim: Importing accurate globe-physics coverage values cannot rescue dome-physics localization. The kill is geometric: under both AE projection conventions and both altitude conventions, every dome-published station sits hundreds to thousands of km from the dome-geometry penumbra path.
other_side_of_the_argument: Per the user-preference directive, captured throughout: anticipated_objections enumerates the three sharpest defender retreats — (a) projection convention, (b) NN-tour start-point, (c) probability prior. Each is countered with the math now visible in the section text. The remaining defender escape routes inherited from EXP-254/EXP-260 (parameters wrong by 97×; non-occultation aetheric mechanism) are acknowledged in the section’s Falsification list and unchanged by these patches — those escapes have their own structural costs the dome would have to pay publicly to invoke. Counter-counter on rejecting these patches: a defender could argue the additions add bulk to a section that already kills cleanly. Net word delta is small (≈160 words on 11.5K) and each addition closes a specific named defender attack the curmudgeon flagged — the cost of NOT patching is letting smart-defender pushback score easy rhetorical points on robustness questions while leaving the load-bearing 0/9 claim untouched.
cross_references: EXP-262 (parallel refraction pre-empt for §4.2.7 — P2 mirrors that pattern for §4.2.7a)

fact_verification.AE_alternate_convention_distances: Verified by halving relation against curmudgeon math_reproduction.consequences_under_alternative_b values.
fact_verification.NN_tour_minimum: Verified ≥ 12,000 km from curmudgeon nn_tour_check (Canary-start = 12,399 km).
fact_verification.probability_calculation: Verified: 0.001^9 = 10^-27; with European-only prior (0.79% of disc) the conditional probability is (0.001/0.0079)^9 ≈ 10^-8.6 ≈ 2.5 × 10^-9.

out_of_scope_explicit: Did NOT address EXP-260 c1 H4 (linear-scaling caveat on 246,000 km), H5 (Canary SNK/GUI footnote), or H6 (NASA central-line sampling source link). Did NOT add EXP-260 c1 S1 (generalization to other dome eclipses). All four are minor or polish; tracker entry asked for the three moderate holes only.

build_impact.sections_json_delta_chars_estimate: +1900 chars (P1: ~1180, P2: ~620, P3: net +320 after replacing the 309-char NN-tour sentence).
build_impact.test_run_required: node build.js html + node test.js (per CLAUDE.md test requirement on docs/* edits via build.js).
build_impact.expected_test_results: All current tests should pass; no DATA blob, count, or rate changes.

notes_for_decider: Three additive text patches via {find,replace} pairs. Each find string was verified unique in current data/sections.json at write time. After applying: close ISS-1613, ISS-1614, ISS-1615. Issue-resolution proposals filed in monitor/analyst/issue-proposals/ with proposal-ISS-NNNN-resolution.json filenames so the decider scanner picks them up.
